revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Hihaizuka Kofun]][[ja:日拝塚古墳]] {{Short description|Historic Site in Kasuga, Fukuoka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hihaizuka Kofun | native_name =日拝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Hihaizuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hihaizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hihaizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.52305555555556|N|130.4386111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|qid=Q47084|lt=Kasuga, Fukuoka|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|23|N|130|26|19|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Hihaizuka Kofun'''|日拝塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimohirasui Minami neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|qid=Q47084|lt=Kasuga|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202886|title=日拝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==Overview== The Hihaizuka Kofun is located on a low terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in the Hakata Plain. This area was the center of the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} {{ill|Nakoku|en|Nakoku|qid=Q600473|lt=Kingdom of Na|1=Nakoku}}, which, according to the ''{{ill|Book of the Later Han|en|Book of the Later Han|qid=Q430428|lt=Book of the Later Han|1=Book of the Later Han}}'', was granted a gold [[king of Na gold seal|imperial seal]] by {{ill|Emperor Guangwu of Han|en|Emperor Guangwu of Han|qid=Q7268|lt=Emperor Guangwu of Han|1=Emperor Guangwu of Han}} in 57 CE {{Cite web | title = The Gold Seal, "Kan no Wa no Na no Kokuo" | url = http://www.fukuoka-art-museum.jp/english/eb/html/eb01/2011/kin_in/kin_in.html |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20181120001209/http://www.fukuoka-art-museum.jp/english/eb/html/eb01/2011/kin_in/kin_in.html |archivedate=20 November 2018 | publisher = Fukuoka Art Museum }} The kingdom is mentioned in [[:s:zh:三國志/卷30|vol. 30]] of the Chinese ''Book of {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|qid=Q320930|lt=Wei|1=Cao Wei}}'' from the ''{{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|en|Records of the Three Kingdoms|qid=Q247318|lt=Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Records of the Three Kingdoms}}'', titled "The Account of the {{ill|Dongyi|en|Dongyi|qid=Q333910|lt=Easterners|1=Dongyi}}: A Note on the [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]]" ({{lang-zh|東夷傳‧倭人條}}), indicating that it continued to exist in the 3rd century. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated towards the west. It has a total length of 41.2 meters, with a 22-meter diameter posterior circular portion with a height of 5.9 meters and a 34-meter wide anterior rectangular portion with a height of 5.4 meters. The tumulus was built in two stages, and no ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' discovered. Although it has disappeared now, there was once a moat surrounding it, and the total length including the moat was about 60 meters.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the center of the posterior portion and is a horizontal-entry stone chamber made of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} blocks that opens to the southwest. It is 3.6 by 2.6 meters, and four meters high with a 4.8 meter long passage that is only one meter wide by 1.5 meters high. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 a large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were discovered, including a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, earring with gold drape, four gold rings and other accessories, and one long iron sword with a ring pommel. Numerous pieces of horse fittings, including three sets of iron ring stirrups, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery were also found. Based on the characteristics of the tumulus and excavated items, the tumulus was constructed in the 6th century. Currently, the excavated items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}, and the Nakuni-no-Oka History Museum in Kasuga City preserves earrings with gold pendants. The tumulus is being maintained as a park. It is located approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hakataminami Station|en|Hakataminami Station|qid=Q906691|lt=Hakataminami Station|1=Hakataminami Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Hakata Minami Line|Hakata Minami Line]]. 日拝塚古墳出土 飾金具.JPG|Metal fittings excavated from Hihaizuka Kofun
{{small|at Tokyo National Museum}} 日拝塚古墳 出土土器.JPG|Sue pottery excavated from Hihaizuka Kofun
{{small| at Tokyo National Museum}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=26 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kasuga.fukuoka.jp/miryoku/history/historymuseum/1002286/1002288/1002292.html Kasuga city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14776 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Hihaizuka Kofun]] {{Commons category|Hihaizuka Kofun}} [[ja:日拝塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q105069738}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
